transcript_facts:
*   The patient reports fatigue for the past six weeks.
*   The patient denies blood in stool.
*   The clinician suspects cervical radiculopathy.
*   The clinician plans a cervical MRI.
*   The patient reports stinging and throbbing in the foot.
*   The patient reports cramping in the calf muscle.
*   The patient reports a red streak along the calf muscle.
*   The patient reports coughing a lot.
*   The patient reports difficulty catching his breath when walking.
*   The patient’s A1c is 6.7.
*   The patient previously had an A1c of 13.
*   The patient rarely has blood sugars over 200.
*   The patient’s blood sugars have been trending in the 3-400 range.
*   The patient hiked to Charlie’s Bunion a week before the ulcer.
*   The patient has new diabetic shoes and inserts.
*   The patient checks his feet every day.
*   The doctor orders a blood supply test for the wound.
*   The doctor will perform a debridement in the office.
*   The doctor will order a culture of the wound.
*   The doctor is concerned about redness moving up the leg.
*   The doctor will monitor the patient closely.
*   The doctor wants to see the patient again in seven days.
*   The doctor wants the patient to follow up with his endocrinologist.
*   The doctor wants to keep the patient’s hemoglobin A1c below 7.
*   The doctor will monitor the patient’s blood sugars.
